items.title: Start with PCA, then UMAP
items.avoid: Jumping straight to UMAP without a linear baseline when exploring new tabular data.
items.prefer: Plot PCA PC1–PC2 first for variance context, then UMAP on the same scaled matrix for non-linear structure.
items.why: PCA is fast and interpretable; UMAP adds value when PCA smears meaningful curved structure.
items.example: StandardScaler → PCA(2) scatter, then StandardScaler → UMAP(2) scatter on identical rows.

items.title: Tune n_neighbors deliberately
items.avoid: Leaving n_neighbors=15 for every dataset regardless of size and goal.
items.prefer: Sweep a small grid (e.g., 10, 30, 50) and pick the layout that matches local vs global questions.
items.why: n_neighbors is the main lever for local/global structure in UMAP's graph construction.
items.example: for k in [15, 30, 100]: fit UMAP(n_neighbors=k, random_state=42) and compare side-by-side plots.

items.title: Use min_dist for cluster tightness
items.avoid: Changing only n_neighbors when clusters overlap unreadably in 2D.
items.prefer: Lower min_dist (0.0–0.1) for tighter islands; raise it when you need more global spacing.
items.why: min_dist controls embedding-space packing without rebuilding the high-D graph.
items.example: Compare min_dist=0.0 vs 0.5 at fixed n_neighbors=30 before publishing a figure.

items.title: Document preprocessing and seeds
items.avoid: Sharing UMAP figures without stating scaling, n_neighbors, min_dist, and random_state.
items.prefer: Record scaler, metric, hyperparameters, and library version alongside plots.
items.why: UMAP layouts are not reproducible or interpretable without the full pipeline context.
items.example: Caption: StandardScaler + UMAP(n_neighbors=30, min_dist=0.1, random_state=42, umap-learn 0.5.5).
